---
title: "3.6 Methods: Passing and Returning References of an Object"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Object variables store references

When an object variable is passed to a method, Java passes a copy of the <span class="term">reference</span> (引用).

The copy still points to the same object.

This topic explains:

::: {.tight-list}
- objects as instance variables
- objects as arguments
- copying parameter objects
- parameters of the same class type
- returning objects
:::

## Objects as Instance Variables

### A class can store another object

```java
public class Person
{
    private String name;
    private Address homeAddress;

    public Person(String personName, Address address)
    {
        name = personName;
        homeAddress = address;
    }
}
```

`homeAddress` is an instance variable whose type is another class.

## Objects as Arguments

### The parameter receives a copy of the reference

![Object reference equality](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-6-methods-passing-and-returning-references-of-an-object/turtleEquality.png){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

If a method receives an object reference, it can call methods on the same object.

```java
public void moveTurtle(Turtle t)
{
    t.forward(50);
}
```

The parameter `t` points to the same turtle object as the argument.

## Code Task

### Address object as an argument

```java
public void updateAddress(Address newAddress)
{
    homeAddress = newAddress;
}
```

Trace:

```java
Address a = new Address("Main St");
Person p = new Person("Kai", a);
p.updateAddress(new Address("Oak St"));
```

After the call, `p` refers to the new `Address` object.

## Copying Parameter Objects

### Copying a reference is not the same as copying an object

```java
public Person(String personName, Address address)
{
    name = personName;
    homeAddress = address;
}
```

This stores the same `Address` object reference.

If the class needs an independent object, it should construct a new one from the parameter's data.

## Same Class Type Parameter

### Methods can compare this object to another object

```java
public boolean sameName(Person other)
{
    return name.equals(other.name);
}
```

Because this method is inside the `Person` class, it can access private instance variables of another `Person` object.

## Returning Objects

### A method can return an object reference

```java
public Address getHomeAddress()
{
    return homeAddress;
}
```

This returns a reference to an `Address` object. Callers can store it:

```java
Address saved = p.getHomeAddress();
```

## Reference Safety

### Returning a mutable object can expose state

If the returned object can be changed, outside code may affect the original object's internal state.

Design choices:

::: {.tight-list}
- return the reference when sharing is intended
- return a copy when protection is needed
- provide specific methods instead of exposing the whole object
:::

## Groupwork Coding Challenge {.fit-small .image-fit}

### Friends and Birthdays

![Friends and birthdays challenge](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-6-methods-passing-and-returning-references-of-an-object/cake.jpg){fig-align="center" width="28%"}

Starter design:

```java
public class Friend
{
    private String name;
    private Date birthday;

    public Friend(String friendName, Date friendBirthday)
    {
        name = friendName;
        birthday = friendBirthday;
    }
}
```

Add methods that compare birthdays or return birthday information.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that object variables store references
- trace what happens when an object reference is passed as an argument
- distinguish copying a reference from copying an object
- use a parameter of the same class type for comparison
- explain what it means for a method to return an object reference
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
